subject: [PATCH] btrfs: fix deadlock between reflink and transaction commit when using flushoncommit

reviews.author: Filipe Manana
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the original description of the deadlock scenario was incomplete and misleading due to an oversimplification of the lock cycle, which actually involves three tasks: clone, commit, and writeback worker.

Reviewer questioned the accuracy of the patch description, pointing out that the stack traces show three tasks involved in the deadlock (clone, commit, and writeback worker), not just two as described, and expressed skepticism about a scenario where the commit task is directly blocked.
reviews.raw_body:
Well it's mentioned in step 1, and extent locking is obvious since we
have to do it for any operation that changes extents in a range, it's
not exclusive to reflinks, we do this everywhere in btrfs. I mentioned
it here in step 2 as a remainder.

---

I didn't mention the commit task waiting for the writeback worker to
simplify things.
I think anyone is able to realize the commit task blocks waiting for
the writeback worker to complete - the stack traces make that obvious.

And no, I haven't seen any case where the commit task is directly
blocked - I don't see how that could happen unless the implementation
of try_to_writeback_inodes_sb() changes and it directly does the
writeback instead of using a worker.

2026-03-25.patch_summary: This patch fixes a deadlock between reflink and transaction commit operations in Btrfs when using the flushoncommit feature. The deadlock occurs because of a lock cycle involving three tasks: clone, commit, and writeback worker. To fix this issue, the patch modifies the locking sequence to prevent the deadlock. The modification ensures that the commit task is not blocked by the writeback worker, thus resolving the deadlock.
